blocks.text: FSSAI Registration
What is FSSAI Registration?

Food Safety Compliance System (FoSCoS) is an enhanced version of Food Licensing and Registration System (FLRS) which was launched in 2012 for issuance of pan-India FSSAI Licenses and Registration. It had evolved incrementally and organically with changing regulatory needs. The technology on which it was built was outdated with technical support no longer available. Over years users complained of slow speed of FLRS and software experts resisted new changes on FLRS thus impeding any further improvement, expansion and innovation in the licensing system. Thus the migration from FLRS to FoSCoS was imperative. FoSCoS is built using latest technology with a vision to have modern on stop pan-India IT platform for food safety regulatory needs. FoSCoS is conceptualized as the umbrella system which is integrated with FSSAI’s other existing IT platforms.

Documents required for FSSAI Registration Process

items: Identity Proof: An identity proof that contains a photograph
Proof of address: Telephone/ Electricity bill of the office address
IE Code: Import/export code if any
List of Directors/Partners: A list of directors or partners
Certificate of Incorporation: A certificate of incorporation (in case of LLP or Company)
MoA and AoA: The MoA and AoA of the business (in case of LLP or Company)
NOC: No Objection Certificate from the local municipality/Local body

For manufacturing units, besides the above-listed documents, the following are also required: The blueprint of warehouse, plant, etc. A list of all machinery Report of a water test List of all food categories

blocks.text: Frequently Asked Questions
What is FSSAI?
FSSAI stands for Food Safety and Standards Authority of India. It is an autonomous body that is established under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India.
What is the FSSAI license number?
The FSSAI license number is a 14-digit number that is issued to a food business operator. It has to be printed on the packaging of the food products.
Is FSSAI license mandatory?
Yes, it is mandatory for all food business operators to obtain an FSSAI license. Non-compliance can result in imprisonment and a penalty of up to Rs. 5 lakhs.
Who needs an FSSAI license?
Every food business operator, including a petty retailer, hawker, temporary stallholder, or a small-scale industry, has to obtain an FSSAI license.
What is the validity of the FSSAI license?
An FSSAI license is issued for a period of one year or five years. It has to be renewed before the expiry of the validity period.
What are the different types of FSSAI registration?
There are three types of FSSAI registration: Basic registration, State license, and Central license. The type of license depends on the annual turnover of the food business operator.
